overview: Ludvig Åberg is making his third Masters start in 2026. After a runner-up performance in his Tournament and major championship debut in 2024, he was tied for the lead with Rory McIlroy and Justin Rose late in the final round in 2025 before finishing seventh. In March, he finished T-3 in the Arnold Palmer Invitational and T-5 in the Players Championship. Last year, he won in San Diego, competed in the Tour Championship for the second consecutive year and represented Europe in the Ryder Cup for the second time. Before turning professional, he was a three-time First Team All-American at Texas Tech, was No. 1 in the World Amateur Golf Ranking and represented Sweden in the Arnold Palmer Cup and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Ludvig Åberg

overview: Daniel Berger is making his seventh Masters start in 2026. Earlier this year, he finished runner-up in the Arnold Palmer Invitational in Orlando and T-6 in Honolulu. In 2025, he had five top-15 finishes worldwide, including runner-up in Phoenix, T-3 in Hilton Head Island and T-11 in Philadelphia. Between 2015 and 2021, he won four times on the PGA Tour, competed in five Tour Championships and represented the United States in both the Presidents Cup and Ryder Cup. Before turning professional, he earned First Team All-America honors for Florida State University and represented his country in the Arnold Palmer Cup in 2013.
name: Daniel Berger

overview: Akshay Bhatia is making his third Masters start in 2026. He has competed in the Tour Championship in each of the past two years. He won the Arnold Palmer Invitational in March and finished T-3 in Phoenix in T-6 in Pebble Beach in February. In 2025, he was T-3 in the Players Championship and T-6 in Memphis. He participated in the first Drive, Chip and Putt National Finals at Augusta National in 2014 and on the day of the 10th competition in 2024, he won in San Antonio to become the first past participant to earn an invitation to the Masters. Before turning professional, he represented the United States in the Junior Presidents Cup, Junior Ryder Cup and Walker Cup.
name: Akshay Bhatia

overview: Keegan Bradley is making his 10th Masters start in 2026. In 2025, his highlights as a competitor included winning in Hartford and finishing inside the top 10 in the PGA Championship, Memorial Tournament and Tour Championship. Last September at Bethpage Black, he became, at the age of 39, the youngest United States Ryder Cup captain since 34-year-old Arnold Palmer in 1963 at East Lake. He has won on the PGA Tour in each of the past four years, and in 2011 he won the PGA Championship at Atlanta Athletic Club in his major championship debut and earned PGA Tour Rookie of the Year honors.
name: Keegan Bradley

overview: Michael Brennan is making his first Masters and second major championship start in 2026. He competed in the 2023 U.S. Open as an amateur. Last October, he won in Utah to earn his first PGA Tour win in just his third career start. Earlier in 2025, he won three times on PGA Tour Americas in Canada and Minnesota to win its season-long points race and earn a Korn Ferry Tour card. In 2024, he had five top-10 finishes in 10 starts on PGA Tour Americas. Before turning professional, he was a two-time All-American at Wake Forest University and represented the United States in the Arnold Palmer Cup.
name: Michael Brennan

overview: Jacob Bridgeman is making his first Masters start in 2026. Earlier this year, he earned his first career PGA Tour win in Los Angeles and finished T-4 in Honolulu, T-5 in the Players Championship and T-8 in Pebble Beach. In 2025, he competed in his first Tour Championship after recording four top-five performances in Palm Beach Gardens, Tampa, Philadelphia and Illinois. As a PGA Tour rookie in 2024, he retained his PGA Tour card following top-15 performances in Greensboro, Mississippi, Mexico and Bermuda late that year. He earned his first PGA Tour card in 2023 through the Korn Ferry Tour. Before turning professional, he was a three-time All-American at Clemson University and represented the United States in the Arnold Palmer Cup.
name: Jacob Bridgeman

overview: Sam Burns is making his fifth Masters start in 2026. He has competed in the Tour Championship in each of the past five years. In 2025, he finished runner-up in the Canadian Open, T-4 in Maryland, T-5 in the Byron Nelson and T-7 in the U.S. Open. He has represented the United States in the previous two Ryder Cups and two Presidents Cups. He earned his first five wins on the PGA Tour in less than 24 months in 2021-2023. Before turning professional, the two-time All-American at Louisiana State University won the Jack Nicklaus Award and represented the United States in the Arnold Palmer Cup in 2017.

overview: Ángel Cabrera is making his 22nd Masters start in 2026. He won the 2009 Masters in a playoff over Kenny Perry and Chad Campbell to become South America's first Tournament champion. He also finished runner-up to Adam Scott at Augusta National in 2013. In 2007, he won the U.S. Open and is one of six to win major championships at Augusta National and Oakmont, with Gene Sarazen, Sam Snead, Ben Hogan, Jack Nicklaus and Dustin Johnson. In 2025, he won two senior major championships with victories in the Tradition in Alabama and the Senior PGA Championship in Maryland.

overview: Brian Campbell is making his second Masters start in 2026. He concluded his Tournament debut last year with a final-round 68 at Augusta National. After winning the Mexico Open in February 2025 for his first career PGA Tour win, he added a second victory in Illinois in July. He won both of those titles in playoffs. In 2024, he earned back his PGA Tour card through the Korn Ferry Tour where he had seven top-10 finishes, including three runner-up performances. Before turning professional, he was a two-time All-American at the University of Illinois and earned low amateur honors in the 2015 U.S. Open at Chambers Bay.
name: Brian Campbell

overview: Patrick Cantlay is making his 10th Masters start in 2026. He earned Low Amateur honors in his Tournament debut in 2012. In 2025, he closed his season by finishing runner-up in the Tour Championship for his fourth top-seven performance at East Lake in the past five years. He has been part of United States teams in each of the past three editions of the Ryder Cup and Presidents Cup. Before turning professional, the two-time All-American at UCLA spent 55 weeks as the top-ranked amateur in the world and represented the United States in both the Arnold Palmer Cup and Walker Cup.
name: Patrick Cantlay

overview: Wyndham Clark is making his third Masters start in 2026. In consecutive weeks in July, he finished T-11 in the Scottish Open and T-4 in The Open at Royal Portrush in Northern Ireland. He also finished T-5 in Houston last year. The 2023 U.S. Open champion at The Los Angeles Country Club in California represented the United States in the 2023 Ryder Cup and both the Olympics and Presidents Cup in 2024. He has also won PGA Tour events at Quail Hollow and Pebble Beach. Before turning professional, he earned conference player of the year awards for both Oklahoma State and Oregon and represented the United States in the Arnold Palmer Cup.

overview: Corey Conners is making his ninth Masters start in 2026. In last year's Tournament, he was inside the top three after each of his first three rounds on his way to his fourth top-10 finish at Augusta National in the past six years. He concluded his 2025 season with a T-4 performance in the Tour Championship following top-six finishes in both the Arnold Palmer Invitational and Players Championship the previous March. He is a two-time Olympian and two-time International Presidents Cup team member for Canada. Before turning professional, he was a two-time All-American at Kent State University in Ohio and represented his country in the Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Corey Conners

overview: Fred Couples is making his 41st Masters start in 2026. He won the 1992 Masters as World No. 1 by two strokes over 1976 champion Raymond Floyd. Last year, he became the 15th player to make at least 40 starts at Augusta National. In addition to his 11 top-10 finishes in this Tournament, he has completed 72 holes in five different decades and became the oldest to make the cut in 2023 at 63 years, 6 months, 5 days of age. The World Golf Hall of Fame member has also won two Players Championships and represented the United States on multiple Ryder Cup and Presidents Cup teams.
name: Fred Couples

overview: Jason Day is making his 15th Masters start in 2026. He has finished inside the top 10 at Augusta National five times, including runner-up in his Tournament debut in 2011, third in 2013 and T-8 in 2025. He finished runner-up in La Quinta in January and T-6 in Houston in March. Other high finishes in 2025 included T-4 in Hartford and T-8 in the Arnold Palmer Invitational. Earlier in his career, he won both the 2015 PGA Championship and 2016 Players Championship, spent 51 weeks as No. 1 in the Official World Golf Ranking, finished second in all four major championships and represented Australia in both the Presidents Cup and the Olympics.
name: Jason Day

overview: Bryson DeChambeau is making his 10th Masters start in 2026. He earned Low Amateur honors in his Tournament debut in 2016 and has finished inside the top six in each of the past two years at Augusta National. In March, he won in Singapore and South Africa in consecutive weeks and last year, he had top-10 performances in both the PGA Championship and The Open and represented the United States in the Ryder Cup. In 2024, he won his second U.S. Open with his victory at Pinehurst No. 2 and was runner-up in the PGA Championship. Before turning professional, he won both the NCAA Division I Men's Championship and U.S. Amateur in 2015 and represented the United States in the Arnold Palmer Cup, Walker Cup and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Bryson DeChambeau

overview: Nicolas Echavarria is making his second Masters start in 2026. Before he made his Tournament debut in 2025, he won the Par 3 Contest. In March, he won in Palm Beach Gardens by two strokes over three players after closing with consecutive bogey-free rounds of 66 to earn his third career PGA Tour victory. He also finished T-8 in the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am in February. In 2025, he had four top-10 finishes worldwide, including runner-up in Honolulu and T-4 in St. Simons Island. He earned his first two PGA Tour wins in Puerto Rico in 2023 and Japan in 2024. Before turning professional, he recorded top-20 finishes in each of the first three editions of the Latin America Amateur Championship.
name: Nicolas Echavarria

overview: Harris English is making his seventh Masters start in 2026. He shot a final-round 68 last year to record both his lowest round and highest finish at Augusta National. He went on to finish runner-up in both the PGA Championship at Quail Hollow and The Open at Royal Portrush, compete in his first Tour Championship since 2021 and represent the United States in the Ryder Cup later in 2025. He also finished inside the top eight in the U.S. Open in 2020, 2021 and 2023. Before turning professional, he was a four-time All-American at the University of Georgia and represented the United States in the Walker Cup.
name: Harris English

overview: Ethan Fang is making his first Masters start in 2026. Last June, he won the Amateur Championship at Royal St George's in England by defeating Ireland's Gavin Tiernan in the final, 1 up. With his victory, he joined Bobby Jones, José María Olazábal and Sergio García as winners of that title. Also last year, the First Team All-American at Oklahoma State University helped his team win the NCAA Division I Men's Golf Championship, finished runner-up in the Western Amateur, competed in The Open at Royal Portrush in Northern Ireland and represented the United States in the Arnold Palmer Cup, Walker Cup and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Ethan Fang

overview: Matt Fitzpatrick is making his 12th Masters start in 2026. In March in consecutive weeks, he finished runner-up in the Players Championship and earned his third career PGA Tour win in Tampa. In 2025, he won the DP World Tour's season-ending event in Dubai and his other high worldwide finishes included T-4 in both the Scottish Open and The Open and T-8 in the PGA Championship. He was also part of his fourth European Ryder Cup team last year. He won both the 2013 U.S. Amateur and 2022 U.S. Open at The Country Club in Brookline, Massachusetts. Before turning professional, he also won the 2012 R&A Boys' Amateur, spent 21 weeks as No. 1 in the World Amateur Golf Rankings and represented Great Britain & Ireland in the Walker Cup.
name: Matt Fitzpatrick

overview: Tommy Fleetwood is making his 10th Masters start in 2026. He has finished inside the top 25 in five of the past eight years at Augusta National. He won last year's Tour Championship at East Lake to both earn his first career PGA Tour victory and win the season-long title. In the two months before his victory, he finished runner-up in Hartford, T-3 in Memphis and T-4 in Maryland. He went on to represent Europe in his fourth Ryder Cup and win a DP World Tour event in India later in 2025. Before turning professional, the 2024 Olympic silver medalist for Great Britain was runner-up in the 2008 Amateur Championship and represented Great Britain & Ireland in the 2009 Walker Cup.
name: Tommy Fleetwood

overview: Ryan Fox is making his third Masters start in 2026 and first since 2024. In 2025, he won in Myrtle Beach in May to earn his first career PGA Tour win and then won the Canadian Open in June to earn his second victory. His wins on the DP World Tour include victories in St Andrews in 2022 and that organization's flagship event in England in 2023. He has represented New Zealand in the Olympics three times in 2016, 2021 and 2024. Before turning professional, he competed in the first three editions of the Asia-Pacific Amateur Championship in 2009-2011 and represented his country in the 2010 Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Ryan Fox

overview: Sergio García is making his 27th Masters start in 2026. He won the 2017 Masters in a playoff over Justin Rose to follow Seve Ballesteros and José María Olazábal as Spaniards to win at Augusta National. In his Tournament debut in 1999 at the age of 19, he earned Low Amateur honors as Olazábal won the second of his two Masters titles. He is one of seven Low Amateurs to win a Green Jacket, with Cary Middlecoff, Jack Nicklaus, Ben Crenshaw, Tiger Woods, Phil Mickelson and Hideki Matsuyama. Earlier this year, he recorded top-10 finishes in Bahrain and Hong Kong. Other highlights of his career before turning professional include winning both the 1997 R&A Boys' Amateur and 1998 Amateur Championship and representing Spain in the Eisenhower Trophy.

overview: Ryan Gerard is making his first Masters start in 2026. He finished second in Honolulu and La Quinta in January and in December, he was runner-up in Mauritius to end 2025 inside the top 50 in the Official World Golf Ranking and earn his first Tournament invitation. Also last year, he won in California in July and was second in San Antonio and T-8 in his PGA Championship debut. In 2024, he won a Korn Ferry Tour event in South Carolina on his way to earning his PGA Tour card for the following season. Before turning professional, he was a two-time All-American at the University of North Carolina.
name: Ryan Gerard

overview: Chris Gotterup is making his first Masters start in 2026. He will join Dave Hill in 1968 and Mark Wilson in 2011 as players since World War II to make their Masters debut with at least four official PGA Tour wins. Since July 2025, he has won the Scottish Open, finished third in The Open at Royal Portrush, competed in his first Tour Championship and started the 2026 season with victories in Honolulu and Phoenix. After earning his PGA Tour card through the Korn Ferry Tour in 2023, he won in Myrtle Beach in 2024. Before turning professional, he competed for four years at Rutgers University in New Jersey and, in his fifth collegiate season for the University of Oklahoma in 2022, he won both the Fred Haskins and Jack Nicklaus Awards.
name: Chris Gotterup

overview: Max Greyserman is making his second Masters start in 2026. In 2025, he recorded multiple runner-up performances on the PGA Tour for the second consecutive year. He was second in Detroit and Japan and also finished T-7 in La Quinta, T-11 in Palm Beach Gardens and T-23 in the U.S. Open at Oakmont last year. In 2024, he was runner-up three times in Minnesota, Greensboro and Japan and also finished fourth in New Orleans and Mexico and T-7 in Houston. He earned his first PGA Tour card through his performance on the Korn Ferry Tour in 2023. Before turning professional, he played collegiate golf at Duke University.
name: Max Greyserman

overview: Ben Griffin is making his first Masters start in 2026. He won a team event with Andrew Novak in New Orleans in April and then won in Fort Worth in May and Mexico in November for his first three victories on the PGA Tour. He also finished runner-up in the Memorial Tournament and in Napa, recorded top-10 finishes in both the U.S. Open and the PGA Championship, competed in his first Tour Championship at East Lake and represented the United States on his first Ryder Cup team last year. Before turning professional, he was a two-time All-American at the University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill.
name: Ben Griffin

overview: Harry Hall is making his first Masters start in 2026. Earlier this year, he finished T-6 in Honolulu and T-9 in the Arnold Palmer Invitational in Orlando. He competed in his first Tour Championship at East Lake in 2025 after recording top-25 finishes in more than half of his PGA Tour starts last season, including sixth-place performances in Fort Worth and Maryland. He won in Kentucky in 2024 to earn his first career win on both the PGA Tour and DP World Tour. Before turning professional, he played collegiate golf at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas and represented his country in both the Arnold Palmer Cup and the Walker Cup.
name: Harry Hall

overview: Brian Harman is making his eighth Masters start in 2026. He competed in the Tour Championship in 2025 for the third time in the past four years after winning in San Antonio and finishing T-3 in Hilton Head Island, eighth in Hartford and T-10 in The Open at Royal Portrush. After winning The Open in 2023 at Royal Liverpool, he went on to represent the United States in both the 2023 Ryder Cup and 2024 Presidents Cup. Before turning professional, he won the 2003 U.S. Junior Amateur, was a three-time All-American at the University of Georgia and represented his country in two Arnold Palmer Cups and two Walker Cups.

After starting 2025 with a win in Dubai, he went on to finish runner-up in St Andrews, record top-five performances in both the U.S. Open and the DP World Tour's flagship event in England and represent Europe in his fourth Ryder Cup last year. Other worldwide wins include the 2017 Italian Open, both the Arnold Palmer Invitational and DP World Tour's flagship event in 2020 and three DP World Tour victories in St Andrews.
name: Tyrrell Hatton

overview: Russell Henley is making his 10th Masters start in 2026. He finished T-6 in the Arnold Palmer Invitational in March as defending champion and T-8 in La Quinta in January. In the past two years, he has finished inside the top four in the Tour Championship twice, recorded top-10 performances in both the U.S. Open and The Open twice and represented the United States in the Presidents Cup in 2024 and the Ryder Cup in 2025. Before turning professional, he was a four-time All-American at the University of Georgia and represented his country in both the Arnold Palmer Cup and Walker Cup.
name: Russell Henley

overview: Jackson Herrington is making his first Masters and first major championship start in 2026. Last August, the University of Tennessee sophomore finished runner-up to Mason Howell in the U.S. Amateur at The Olympic Club in California. Notable runners-up in the U.S. Amateur to go on to win Low Amateur honors in the following year's Masters include Ben Crenshaw in 1973, Patrick Cantlay in 2012, Doug Ghim in 2018 and Neal Shipley two years ago. In 2024, he earned Tennessee Men's and Junior Player of the Year honors, finished runner-up with Blades Brown in the U.S. Amateur Four-Ball Championship in Pennsylvania and reached match play in both the U.S. Amateur and Western Amateur.
name: Jackson Herrington

overview: Nicolai Højgaard is making his third Masters start in 2026. He competed in 2025 alongside his brother Rasmus as the first pair of twins to compete in the same Tournament. Both he and his brother have won multiple times on the DP World Tour and they finished second together in a team event in New Orleans last April. In his debut at Augusta National in 2024, he briefly led through 10 holes in the third round before finishing T-16. Earlier this year, he finished runner-up in Houston, T-3 in Phoenix, T-4 in Dubai and T-6 in Palm Beach Gardens. In 2025, he had five top-10 finishes worldwide, including runner-up in England, T-3 in Abu Dhabi and T-4 in the Scottish Open. He has represented his country in the Ryder Cup, Olympics and Eisenhower Trophy.

overview: Brandon Holtz is making his first Masters and first major championship start in 2026. Last September while competing in his first USGA championship, he won the U.S. Mid-Amateur at Troon Country Club in Arizona with a 3&2 victory over Jeg Coughlin III. With his victory, he received the Robert T. Jones Jr. Memorial Trophy and earned places in the fields for both the Masters and the U.S. Open in 2026. Jay Sigel in 1988 and Stewart Hagestad in 2017 both earned Low Amateur honors in the Masters after winning this title the previous year. After playing college basketball at Illinois State University, he played golf professionally before later regaining his amateur status.
name: Brandon Holtz

Before turning professional, he was a two-time All-American at the University of California, Berkeley and in 2013, he won the NCAA Division I Men's Individual Championship and represented the United States in the Walker Cup.

overview: Mason Howell is making his first Masters start in 2026. overview: Fifa Laopakdee is making his first Masters and first major championship start in 2026. He won the Asia-Pacific Amateur in Dubai in October to become Thailand's first champion. He is the first amateur representing Thailand to compete in the Masters. The Arizona State University junior also won the individual gold medal in the Southeast Asian Games in Thailand by 11 strokes in 2025. He has represented his country in both the Eisenhower Trophy and Junior Presidents Cup.
name: Fifa Laopakdee

overview: Min Woo Lee is making his fifth Masters start in 2026. Earlier this year, he finished runner-up in the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am and T-6 in the Arnold Palmer Invitational and last March, he won in Houston to earn his first career PGA Tour victory. name: Min Woo Lee

overview: Haotong Li is making his third Masters start in 2026 and first since 2019. Last July, he finished T-4 in The Open at Royal Portrush following four under-par rounds. He earned his PGA Tour card for 2026 through last season's DP World Tour after winning in Qatar and finishing runner-up in Turkey, T-4 in Belgium and China and T-8 in the season-ending event in Dubai. Other career highlights include winning the Dubai Desert Classic in 2018 and representing his country in the Olympics in Brazil in 2016 and the Presidents Cup in Australia in 2019.
name: Haotong Li

overview: Shane Lowry is making his 11th Masters start in 2026. He finished runner-up in both Philadelphia and the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am last year to reach the Tour Championship for the second consecutive edition. He also competed in his third Ryder Cup in 2025 and made the putt to retain the trophy for Europe. The 2019 Open champion at Royal Portrush has top-four finishes in all four majors, including runner-up in the 2016 U.S. Open and T-3 in the 2022 Masters. He has also represented his country in both the Olympics and Eisenhower Trophy. Before turning professional, he won the Irish Open in 2009 as an amateur.
name: Shane Lowry

overview: Robert MacIntyre is making his fourth Masters start in 2026. He has competed in the Tour Championship in each of the past two years. After winning both the Canadian and Scottish Opens in 2024, he won in St Andrews, had second-place performances in the U.S. Open and in Maryland and finished inside the top 10 in The Open and the Players Championship in 2025. He also represented Europe in his second Ryder Cup last year. His DP World Tour victories also include the 2022 Italian Open. Before turning professional, he finished runner-up in the 2016 Amateur Championship and represented his country in both the Walker Cup and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Robert MacIntyre

overview: Hideki Matsuyama is making his 15th Masters start in 2026. He won the 2021 Masters by one stroke over Will Zalatoris to become Japan's first male major champion. His connection to Augusta National also includes earning Low Amateur honors in 2011 and winning the Asia-Pacific Amateur Championship in both 2010 and 2011. Since 2015, he has made the cut in each of the past 11 Tournaments and has nine top-25 finishes. He finished runner-up in Phoenix in February and last year, he won in Maui and competed in his 11th Tour Championship. He also won the Japan Open in 2016, earned a bronze medal in the Olympics in 2024 in Paris and competed on each of the past six International Presidents Cup teams.
name: Hideki Matsuyama

overview: Matt McCarty is making his second Masters start in 2026. He was inside the top five through 36 holes and finished T-14 in his Tournament debut last year. name: Matt McCarty

overview: Rory McIlroy is making his 18th Masters start in 2026. Also last year, he won the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am, Players Championship and Irish Open, was a member of his eighth European Ryder Cup team and secured his seventh season-long title on the DP World Tour. He has also represented his country in the Olympics, Walker Cup and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Rory McIlroy

overview: Tom McKibbin is making his first Masters start in 2026. He won the Hong Kong Open in November by seven strokes in wire-to-wire fashion to earn his first Asian Tour win. name: Tom McKibbin

overview: Maverick McNealy is making his second Masters start in 2026. Before turning professional, the three-time All-American at Stanford University won the Fred Haskins, Ben Hogan and Jack Nicklaus Awards, spent 47 weeks as No. 1 in the World Amateur Golf Ranking and represented the United States in the Arnold Palmer Cup, Walker Cup and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Maverick McNealy

overview: Phil Mickelson is making his 33rd Masters start in 2026. He is one of eight to win the Masters at least three times. overview: Alex Noren is making his fifth Masters start in 2026 and first since 2023. name: Jon Rahm

overview: Aaron Rai is making his second Masters start in 2026. In November, he won in Abu Dhabi to earn his third career DP World Tour win and add to his victories in the 2018 Hong Kong Open and 2020 Scottish Open. Other high finishes in 2025 included T-3 in the DP World Tour's flagship event in England, T-4 in the Mexico Open and T-5 in Greensboro. He also recorded top-20 performances in the Arnold Palmer Invitational, Players Championship and PGA Championship last year. In 2024, he earned his first career PGA Tour win in Greensboro, finished runner-up in Detroit and competed in his first Tour Championship at East Lake.
name: Aaron Rai

overview: Patrick Reed is making his 13th Masters start in 2026. He won the 2018 Masters by one stroke over Rickie Fowler. He made eagle on No. 17 in last year's final round to record his sixth top-12 performance in the past eight Masters. Earlier this year, he won in Dubai and Qatar and finished runner-up in Bahrain. Other high finishes in 2025 included runner-up in an Asian Tour event in Macao, China and T-3 in the DP World Tour's flagship event in England. He has represented the United States in the Olympics, Ryder Cup and Presidents Cup and his other worldwide wins include the 2024 Hong Kong Open. Before turning professional, the two-time All-American for Augusta University helped his team win the NCAA Division I Men's Golf Championship title in both 2010 and 2011.
name: Patrick Reed

overview: Kristoffer Reitan is making his first Masters start in 2026. He will join Viktor Hovland as golfers representing Norway to compete in the Masters. Last year, he earned his PGA Tour card for 2026 after winning DP World Tour events in Belgium and South Africa and recording top-five finishes in Austria, China, Germany, Scotland and the French Open. In 2024, he earned his DP World Tour card through the European Challenge Tour where he closed his season by winning its Grand Final in Spain. Before turning professional, he represented Norway in the Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Kristoffer Reitan

overview: Davis Riley is making his second Masters start in 2026. He shot 69 in both the second and final rounds last year at Augusta National to finish inside the top 25 in his Tournament debut. His 2025 was highlighted by a second-place performance in the PGA Championship at Quail Hollow. He also finished T-6 in the Puerto Rico Open and seventh in Tampa last year. He earned his first two PGA Tour wins in New Orleans in 2023 and in Fort Worth in 2024. Before turning professional, the two-time All-American at the University of Alabama finished runner-up in both the 2013 and 2014 U.S. Junior Amateur and represented the United States in the Arnold Palmer Cup.
name: Davis Riley

overview: Justin Rose is making his 21st Masters start in 2026. He made 10 birdies in his final-round 66 last year before losing in a playoff to Rory McIlroy. He is one of nine players in Masters history to finish runner-up at least three times at Augusta National. He has 15 top-25 finishes in his 20 Tournament starts. In February, he won in San Diego by seven strokes in wire-to-wire fashion. Other 2025 highlights included winning in Memphis and playing in his seventh Ryder Cup for Europe. He also won the 2013 U.S. Open at Merion and won a gold medal for Great Britain in the 2016 Olympics in Brazil. Before turning professional, he represented Great Britain & Ireland in the 1997 Walker Cup at the age of 17.
name: Justin Rose

overview: Xander Schauffele is making his ninth Masters start in 2026. He has finished inside the top 10 at Augusta National in five of the past seven years. He finished third in the Players Championship in March and last year, he won in Japan, had top-12 performances in both the U.S. Open and The Open and was part of his third United States Ryder Cup team. The 2021 Olympic Gold Medalist in Tokyo won both the PGA Championship and The Open in 2024 and has finished inside the top 15 in each of his nine U.S. Open starts. His worldwide wins also include the 2017 Tour Championship and 2022 Scottish Open.
name: Xander Schauffele

overview: Scottie Scheffler is making his seventh Masters start in 2026. He won the Masters in 2022 and 2024 and has not finished outside the top 20 at Augusta National. Earlier this year, he won in La Quinta and finished inside the top four in Phoenix and Pebble Beach. He had top-10 performances in 17 of his 20 starts on the PGA Tour in 2025 and his six victories included the PGA Championship at Quail Hollow Club and The Open at Royal Portrush. The 2024 Olympic Gold Medalist in Paris has earned each of his PGA Tour wins since February 2022. Before turning professional, the three-time All-American at the University of Texas won the 2013 U.S. Junior Amateur and represented the United States in the Walker Cup and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Scottie Scheffler

overview: Charl Schwartzel is making his 17th Masters start in 2026. He won the 75th Masters in 2011 by two strokes over Jason Day and Adam Scott with consecutive closing birdies on Nos. 15, 16, 17 and 18. His victory came 50 years after fellow South African Gary Player in 1961 became the first international champion with the first of his three Tournament titles. He also finished third in the 2017 Masters. Other career highlights include winning the 2007 Spanish Open and playing on four International Presidents Cup teams. Before turning professional, he represented his country in the Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Charl Schwartzel

overview: Adam Scott is making his 25th Masters start in 2026. He won the 2013 Masters in a playoff over Ángel Cabrera to become Australia's first champion at Augusta National. His five Tournament top-10 finishes also include a runner-up performance in 2011. He has top-four finishes in all four major championships, including runner-up in the 2012 Open at Royal Lytham & St Annes. In 2025, his T-12 finish in the U.S. Open included a third-round 67 at Oakmont. Late last year, he recorded top-12 finishes in the Japan Open, Australian Open and Australian PGA Championship. Other career highlights include winning the 2004 Players Championship and 2009 Australian Open and representing the International team in each of the past 11 Presidents Cups.
name: Adam Scott

overview: Vijay Singh is making his 32nd Masters start in 2026. He won the 2000 Masters by three strokes over Ernie Els for the second of his three major titles. It was also the first of his six top-10 finishes over seven years at Augusta National, from 2000-2006. He finished T-8 in the Senior PGA Championship in 2025. The World Golf Hall of Fame member earned 22 of his 34 victories on the PGA Tour after his 40th birthday. Other worldwide wins include the South African Open in 1997 and the PGA Championship in 1998 and 2004. He has represented Fiji in both the Presidents Cup and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Vijay Singh

overview: Cameron Smith is making his 10th Masters start in 2026. He has top-10 performances in five of the past eight Tournaments, including runner-up to Dustin Johnson in 2020, T-3 in 2022 and T-5 in 2018. In 2022, he won the 150th edition of The Open at St Andrews to join the list of champions on the Old Course that includes Bobby Jones, Sam Snead, Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo and Tiger Woods. Last December, he finished runner-up in the Australian Open at The Royal Melbourne Golf Club. Other career highlights include winning the 2022 Players Championship and representing Australia in both the Olympics and Presidents Cup. Before turning professional, he won the 2013 Australian Amateur and represented his country in the Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Cameron Smith

overview: J.J. Spaun is making his third Masters start in 2026. He won last year's U.S. Open at Oakmont by two strokes over Robert MacIntyre with closing birdies on Nos. 17 and 18. He also finished runner-up in Memphis, Palm Beach Gardens and the Players Championship, competed in his first Tour Championship and was part of his first United States Ryder Cup team in 2025. He retained his PGA Tour card in 2024 with the help of late-year top-10 finishes in Minnesota, Greensboro and Japan. In 2022, he won in San Antonio to earn his first career PGA Tour win and first invitation to the Masters before finishing inside the top 25 in his Tournament debut seven days later.
name: J.J. Spaun

overview: Jordan Spieth is making his 13th Masters start in 2026. He won the 2015 Masters by four strokes over Phil Mickelson and Justin Rose at the age of 21 in wire-to-wire fashion. He has finished inside the top four at Augusta National six times, including runner-up in both his Tournament debut in 2014 and 2016. He had top-seven performances in both the Byron Nelson and the Memorial Tournament last year. He has also won both the 2015 U.S. Open and 2017 Open and represented the United States in five Ryder Cups and four Presidents Cups. Before turning professional, he represented his country in the Walker Cup in 2011 and helped the University of Texas win the NCAA Division I Men's Golf Championship in 2012.
name: Jordan Spieth

overview: Samuel Stevens is making his first Masters start in 2026. He finished fifth in Houston and T-6 in La Quinta earlier this year. Last season on the PGA Tour, he was runner-up in San Diego and Minnesota and third in the Byron Nelson in Texas. He also recorded his best finishes in both the PGA Championship and U.S. Open and closed 2025 with a T-7 performance last November in St. Simons Island. In 2024, he recorded four top-10 finishes on the PGA Tour in New Orleans, Myrtle Beach, Detroit and Mexico and was also runner-up in a Korn Ferry Tour event in his hometown of Wichita, Kansas. Before turning professional, he played collegiate golf at Oklahoma State University where his teammates included future PGA Tour winners Wyndham Clark, Austin Eckroat, Viktor Hovland and Matthew Wolff.
name: Samuel Stevens

overview: Sepp Straka is making his fifth Masters start in 2026. In his Tournament debut in 2022, he joined Bernd Wiesberger as the second golfer representing Austria to compete in the Masters. He finished runner-up in Pebble Beach in February and T-8 in the Players Championship in March. Last year, he won twice in La Quinta and Philadelphia to reach the Tour Championship for the fourth consecutive edition. He was also a part of the winning European Ryder Cup team for the second time in 2025. In both 2021 in Tokyo and 2024 in Paris, he represented Austria in the Olympics. Before turning professional, he was an All-American at the University of Georgia.
name: Sepp Straka

overview: Nick Taylor is making his fourth Masters start in 2026. He competed in his second Tour Championship in 2025 after winning in Honolulu for his fifth career PGA Tour victory. In 2024, he won in Phoenix and represented Canada in the Olympics in Paris. In 2023, he became the first Canadian since 1954 to win his national open. Before turning professional, the three-time All-American at the University of Washington spent 20 weeks as No. 1 in the World Amateur Golf Ranking, won both the Canadian Junior and Canadian Amateur Championships and represented his country in the Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Nick Taylor

overview: Justin Thomas is making his 11th Masters start in 2026. He competed in his ninth Tour Championship in 2025 after a win in Hilton Head Island and runner-up performances in La Quinta, Tampa and Philadelphia. He was also part of his fourth United States Ryder Cup team last year. His other worldwide wins include both the 2017 and 2022 PGA Championship as well as the 2021 Players Championship. Before turning professional, the two-time First Team All-American at the University of Alabama helped his team win the 2013 NCAA Division I Men's Golf Championship and represented his country in the Arnold Palmer Cup, Walker Cup and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Justin Thomas

overview: Sami Valimaki is making his first Masters start in 2026. He will join 2001 and 2015 Tournament participant Mikko Ilonen as golfers representing Finland to compete in the Masters. Last year, he won in St. Simons Island to become Finland's first winner on the PGA Tour and finished runner-up in Switzerland and Mexico, fourth in Houston and T-7 in Myrtle Beach. In 2024, he finished runner-up in the Mexico Open and T-9 in the Irish Open. He won on the DP World Tour in 2020 in Oman and 2023 in Qatar and represented his country in both the Olympics and Eisenhower Trophy.
name: Sami Valimaki

overview: Bubba Watson is making his 18th Masters start in 2026. He is one of 18 players to win at Augusta National multiple times. In 2012, he defeated Louis Oosthuizen in a playoff following a memorable shot from the trees on the second extra hole on No. 10. Then like Horton Smith, Ben Hogan, Arnold Palmer, Jack Nicklaus, Phil Mickelson and Scottie Scheffler, he earned his second victory two years after his first when he won in 2014 by three strokes over Jonas Blixt and Jordan Spieth. He has represented the United States in the Ryder Cup, Presidents Cup and Olympics. Before turning professional, he was an All-American at the University of Georgia.
name: Bubba Watson

overview: Mike Weir is making his 27th Masters start in 2026. He won the 2003 Masters in a playoff over Len Mattiace to become Canada's first Tournament champion and its first male major champion. He also became the first left-handed major champion since Bob Charles won the 1963 Open. The five-time International Presidents Cup team member was captain in 2024 at The Royal Montreal Golf Club in Canada. His other worldwide wins include the 2001 Tour Championship at Champions Golf Club in Texas. Before turning professional, he was an All-American at Brigham Young University in Utah.

overview: Danny Willett is making his 12th Masters start in 2026. He won the 2016 Masters by three strokes over Lee Westwood and defending champion Jordan Spieth. He became England's first Tournament champion since Nick Faldo won the third of his three titles in 1996. He has won both the flagship and season-ending events on the DP World Tour and has competed in both the Olympics and Ryder Cup. Before turning professional, he spent 12 weeks as No. 1 in the World Amateur Golf Ranking, won both the English and Spanish Amateur Championships and represented Great Britain & Ireland in the Walker Cup.

overview: Gary Woodland is making his 13th Masters start and first since 2024. In consecutive weeks in March, he finished T-14 in Tampa and won in Houston. His five-stroke victory in Texas was his fifth on the PGA Tour and first since 2019 when he won the U.S. Open at Pebble Beach to join its list of major champions with Jack Nicklaus, Lanny Wadkins, Tom Watson, Tom Kite, Tiger Woods and Graeme McDowell. He received the Ben Hogan Award from the Golf Writers Association of America in 2024 and the PGA Tour Courage Award in 2025 for returning to competitive golf after undergoing surgery to remove a brain lesion in September 2023.

overview: Cameron Young is making his fifth Masters start in 2026. He has top-10 performances at Augusta National in two of the past three years, finishing T-7 in 2023 and T-9 in 2024. Earlier this year, he won the Players Championship and finished T-3 in the Arnold Palmer Invitational and T-7 in Los Angeles. In 2025, he earned his first career PGA Tour win in Greensboro, finished T-4 in the Canadian Open, U.S. Open and Tour Championship and represented the United States in his first Ryder Cup. Before earning his first PGA Tour win, he had finished inside the top three nine times, including runner-up in The Open and T-3 in the PGA Championship in 2022. He was a two-time All-American at Wake Forest University in North Carolina.
